articles.title: Question of faith beyond judicial review: Govt to Supreme Court on Sabarimala
articles.description: Sabarimala row: The government also advanced a broader doctrinal claim: that the attributes of a deity are not open to judicial review.

articles.title: RBI moves to curb rupee volatility after sharp FY26 slide
articles.description: The Reserve Bank of India is tightening oversight of foreign-exchange markets and non-deliverable forward (NDF) norms to curb rupee volatility. Recent directives cap banks' net open foreign-exchange positions and prohibit offering rupee NDF contracts, forcing unwinding of existing positions and preventing new ones. These measures aim to stabilize the rupee by reducing arbitrage opportunities and offshore influence.

articles.title: Names of 45% of ‘discrepancy’ voters found in West Bengal SIR may be cut ahead of assembly polls
articles.description: The 19 tribunals — which were set up to hear the appeals of the people rejected during the adjudication process — are yet to become fully operational.

articles.title: Hegseth compares Easter rescue of US airman to resurrection of Christ as Pope Leo rejects holy war rhetoric
articles.description: Pete Hegseth drew biblical parallels between Easter Sunday rescue of downed US airman and Resurrection of Christ, while President Trump claimed divine support for Iran war. Pope Leo XIV has repeatedly pushed back, warning that Christianity cannot be used to justify warfare.

articles.title: Centre eyes eco-friendly sachets to tackle plastic litter surge
articles.description: This comes against the backdrop of the government’s ban on single-use plastic and assumes significance as India generates around 3.5 million tonnes of plastic waste annually, according to the Central Pollution Control Board. Of this, around 40% of plastic waste is littered and remains uncollected.

articles.title: Supreme Court hands over probe into Malda mob violence to NIA
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Monday directed the National Investigation Agency (NIA) to probe into the incident of mob violence in West Bengal’s Malda district

articles.title: Ukraine's Zelensky Says He Stands By Ceasefire Proposal To Russia
articles.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Monday that he stood by a proposal made to Russia for a ceasefire contingent on Moscow halting all attacks on energy infrastructure.

articles.title: From margins to muscle, BJP’s Kerala push intensifies
articles.description: Since its inception in 1980, the BJP has got just one MLA (2016) and one MP (2024) from Kerala

articles.title: 10-year benchmark sovereign bond yield falls 9 bps
articles.description: Indian sovereign bond yields saw a significant drop on Monday. This retreat followed reports of a potential de-escalation in the West Asia war. Expectations of a peace framework between the US and Iran boosted market sentiment. Additionally, a lower-than-anticipated state borrowing plan for the April-June quarter provided further support. Bond traders are now awaiting the central bank's policy rate decision.

articles.title: Around 95% of voters removed after Bengal SIR in Nandigram are Muslims, shows data
articles.description: In Nandigram, West Bengal, 95.5% of voter deletions are Muslims, raising concerns about electoral fairness ahead of upcoming elections.

articles.title: Bangladesh plans to buy wheat from India as global supply tightens
articles.description: Bangladesh seeks to import wheat from India amid global supply disruptions. Traders expect significant purchases as India lifts its export ban.

articles.title: IAF chief begins US visit to bolster defence ties
articles.description: The Chief of Air Staff, Air Chief Marshal AP Singh, began his week-long visit to the United States on Monday to bolster defence ties

articles.title: Nifty aggregate profit to fall in Q4 but expect a better FY27
articles.description: Nifty 50 companies are projected to see a 1.1% dip in net profit for the March quarter due to one-off gains in the previous year and lower expected profits from banking, pharma, and IT sectors. Revenue is anticipated to grow 8.3%, with operating margins expected to contract by 70 basis points.

articles.title: Jewellery stocks rise on talk of cut in base import price
articles.description: Gold jeweller stocks surged on Monday following positive business updates and a reduction in precious metal import prices. Analysts anticipate sustained demand driven by the wedding season and upcoming festivals, with companies like Senco Gold and PC Jeweller reporting significant year-on-year revenue growth.

articles.title: Air India and IndiGo issue travel advisory for Dubai, Jeddah and Abu Dhabi flights on 7 April
articles.description: Air India, Air India Express and IndiGo have issued travel advisories for Middle East flights on 7 April 2026. Here is what passengers travelling to Dubai, Jeddah, Abu Dhabi and other West Asia destinations need to know about rebooking, refunds and revised schedules.

articles.title: D-Street grasps at ceasefire straw, rebounds 1% from early losses
articles.description: Indian stock indices surged over 1% on Monday, recovering from early losses as a West Asia ceasefire proposal boosted sentiment. The Nifty closed at 22,968.25 and the Sensex at 74,106.85, driven by short covering and stable crude oil prices. Broader market indices also saw gains, indicating a potential rebound.

articles.title: Govt plans push for electric stoves to reduce LPG dependence

articles.title: Independent directors key to fixing governance gaps: Pandey
articles.description: Sebi chairperson Tuhin Kanta Pandey called for a fundamental shift in how independent directors function, stating that boardroom independence in India is often procedural and lacks effective oversight. He emphasized the need for boards to move beyond compliance and foster diverse perspectives and constructive dissent, focusing on improving the quality of board engagement rather than adding new regulations.

articles.title: RBI moves to curb rupee volatility after sharp FY26 slide
articles.description: The Reserve Bank of India is tightening oversight of foreign-exchange markets and non-deliverable forward (NDF) norms to curb rupee volatility. Recent directives cap banks' net open foreign-exchange positions and prohibit offering rupee NDF contracts, forcing unwinding of existing positions and preventing new ones. These measures aim to stabilize the rupee by reducing arbitrage opportunities and offshore influence.

articles.description: This comes against the backdrop of the government’s ban on single-use plastic and assumes significance as India generates around 3.5 million tonnes of plastic waste annually, according to the Central Pollution Control Board. Of this, around 40% of plastic waste is littered and remains uncollected.

articles.title: Supreme Court hands over probe into Malda mob violence to NIA
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Monday directed the National Investigation Agency (NIA) to probe into the incident of mob violence in West Bengal’s Malda district

articles.title: Ukraine's Zelensky Says He Stands By Ceasefire Proposal To Russia
articles.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Monday that he stood by a proposal made to Russia for a ceasefire contingent on Moscow halting all attacks on energy infrastructure.

articles.title: From margins to muscle, BJP’s Kerala push intensifies
articles.description: Since its inception in 1980, the BJP has got just one MLA (2016) and one MP (2024) from Kerala

topStories.title: Question of faith beyond judicial review: Govt to Supreme Court on Sabarimala
topStories.description: Sabarimala row: The government also advanced a broader doctrinal claim: that the attributes of a deity are not open to judicial review.

topStories.title: IAF chief begins US visit to bolster defence ties
topStories.description: The Chief of Air Staff, Air Chief Marshal AP Singh, began his week-long visit to the United States on Monday to bolster defence ties

topStories.title: Iran war live: Trump warns of attacks as Hormuz deal deadline nears
topStories.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says US forces are carrying out more strikes on Iran than any day since start of war.

topStories.title: Trump's deadline nears - with little indication Iran is on board
topStories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

topStories.title: "Don't Care About Critics": Trump On Expletive-Laded Post Against Iran
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump brushed off criticism of his expletive-laden references to causing destruction in Iran if they failed to make a deal to end the war.

topStories.title: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
topStories.description: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

topStories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
topStories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

topStories.title: Trump signals ‘active negotiation partner’ in Iran talks, hints at progress in negotiations
topStories.description: “I can't talk about ceasefire. But I can tell you that we have an active, willing participant on the other side. They would like to be able to make a deal. I can't say any more than that,” Trump told reporters.

topStories.title: Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
topStories.description: Protesters gathered outside the US embassy in Tel Aviv, calling for an end to the war with Iran and Lebanon.

topStories.description: Influential in at least 35 of the 126 seats in Assam, the ‘tea tribes’ have gravitated towards the BJP since 2014; the Congress is counting on the State government’s ‘failure’ to increase wages and provide ST reservation to the community

dailyNews.description: Government informs the court about the constitution of an expert committee comprising former apex court judge Justice (retired) Indu Malhotra, senior advocate KK Venugopal and Hemvati Nandan Bahuguna Garhwal University vice chancellor Prakash Singh, to finalise the NCERT curriculum on Legal Studies for Class 8 and other grades.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court directs CBI to probe ‘contracts awarded’ to Arunachal Pradesh CM Pema Khandu’s family
dailyNews.description: Petitioners alleged that there was rampant corruption, nepotism and dispensing with a transparent and open tender system in the award of contracts for 10 years between January 2015 till December 31, 2025

dailyNews.description: Steve Bannon, an ally of US President Donald Trump, served a four-month prison sentence after his 2022 conviction.

dailyNews.title: US ramps up threats if Iran doesn’t meet deadline — ‘Every bridge will be decimated by 12 o’clock tomorrow night’
dailyNews.description: “We have a plan, because of the power of our military, where every bridge in Iran will be decimated by 12 o’clock tomorrow night,” Trump said, describing the scale of potential US strikes.

dailyNews.description: Donald Trump claimed the US has “numerous intercepts” of Iranians pleading for a continuation of bombing.

dailyNews.title: Trump aiming to 'subvert the midterms' and 'sow doubt' about results, ABC's Stephanopolous reports
dailyNews.description: ABC anchor George Stephanopoulos claimed President Donald Trump is trying to sow doubt about the 2026 midterms with his executive order on mail-in ballots.

dailyNews.title: Trump roasts Biden over autopen use in viral interaction with kids at White House Easter Egg Roll
dailyNews.description: President Trump turned the annual White House Easter Egg Roll into a comedy session, joking about selling his autographs and taking shots at President Biden's autopen use.

dailyNews.title: White House fires back at 'SNL' after Michael Che cracks Trump assassination joke during 'Weekend Update'
dailyNews.description: The White House knocked NBC's "Saturday Night Live" after "Weekend Update" anchor Michael Che made an assassination joke about President Donald Trump.

dailyNews.title: Iran’s 10-Point Proposal Demands an End to Attacks and Sanctions
dailyNews.description: As President Trump’s deadline for new attacks loomed, Iran conveyed its conditions through Pakistani intermediaries.
